text.title: GitHub Actions — Caching and Artifacts Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Cache keys MUST hash the lockfile and the OS+language version (e.g. `${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}`). Random or branch-name keys are rejected.
rule.rationale: Non-deterministic keys cause cache poisoning or constant misses; deterministic keys give predictable hit ratio.

rule.statement: Cache restore MUST declare a `restore-keys` fallback that prefixes-matches older keys, enabling partial reuse when lockfile changes incrementally.
rule.rationale: Without restore-keys every lockfile change is a cold cache; partial reuse cuts the cold-cache cost in half.

rule.statement: Cross-job handoff of build outputs (binary, html report, container image manifest) MUST use `actions/upload-artifact` / `download-artifact`, not the cache. Cache is for inputs; artifacts are for outputs.
rule.rationale: Conflating cache with artifact leads to lost data (cache evicted) or stale data (artifact treated as cache).

rule.statement: Docker builds MUST use `docker/build-push-action` with `cache-from` + `cache-to` targeting either GHA cache or registry cache. Plain `docker build` without layer cache is rejected for pipelines >30s.
rule.rationale: Without layer cache, base layers rebuild on every CI run, wasting minutes per build.

rule.statement: Artifact retention MUST be set explicitly (default 90d is wasteful). Recommended: 7d for PR artifacts, 30d for main, 90d for releases.
rule.rationale: Default retention bloats storage; explicit retention keeps the cost predictable.

rule.statement: Skip when all builds <2min OR cache key cannot be deterministic OR security policy forbids cache for the secret class.
rule.rationale: Below threshold caching wastes engineering; without deterministic keys it actively harms; secret classes need different controls.
